name: The Subterranean Pump Room
description: The limestone-lined pit beneath the tower's foundation, housing the two Deane duplex steam pumps and their connecting pipe manifold — damp, low-ceilinged, and lit by a single iron lantern hanging from a beam.
atmosphericDetails: The rhythmic clack-hiss of the steam pistons is felt as much as heard — a chest-deep percussion that makes conversation difficult.
A packing gland on the number-two pump is weeping steadily, the cold water spraying in a fine mist that has soaked the nearest wall.
The coal-feed valve handle has a carved wooden shim jammed into its seat — crude, deliberate, and still in place.
